chunks.text: IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, BENCH AT SUKKUR Const. Petition No. D-1697 of 2021 Const. Petition No. D-1119 of 2022 Const. Petition No. D-1401 of 2022 Const. Petition No. D-1865 of 2019(Disposed of) 05-12-2023 M/s Ashique Illahi Sundrani, Ali Gul Abbasi, Abdul Naeem Pirzada, Sikandar Ali Junejo, Abdul Rehman Khakhrani and Muhammad Zohaib Azam, Advocates for petitioners. Mr. Wazir Ahmed Ghoto, Advocate for respondents-M.C, Ghotki. M/s Ali Raza Baloch and Ghulam Mustafa Abro, Assistant A.Gs Sindh a/w Qurban Ali Soomro, Deputy Secretary, Local Government Dept. ********** All these petitions are pending since 2021 filed by petitioners, claiming to be employees of the Municipal Committee, Ghotki for seeking directions for release of their salaries. During pendency of these petitions, when certain questions were raised over authenticity of appointment of the petitioners from the side of respondents, the Commissioner Sukkur Division was directed to hold an enquiry and submit a report in this regard. He submitted the report on 21.09.2023, a copy of which has already been supplied to learned counsel for petitioners. All the counsel and some of the petitioners present in person in Court, submit that since the enquiry report is adverse to their rights as employees of the Municipal Committee, Ghotki and it has been held that their appointments are not genuine, they intend to challenge the same before the Sindh Appellate Tribunal of Local Council and do not press these petitions and pending application(s) with a permission to avail such remedy in accordance with law. Accordingly, in view of above, these petitions along with pending application(s) are dismissed as not pressed leaving the petitioners to avail remedy before the proper forum in accordance with law.

All the counsel and some of the petitioners present in person in Court, submit that since the enquiry report is adverse to their rights as employees of the Municipal Committee, Ghotki and it has been held that their appointments are not genuine, they intend to challenge the same before the Sindh Appellate Tribunal of Local Council and do not press these petitions and pending application(s) with a permission to avail such remedy in accordance with law. Accordingly, in view of above, these petitions along with pending application(s) are dismissed as not pressed leaving the petitioners to avail remedy before the proper forum in accordance with law. Office to place a signed copy of this order in captioned connected matters. JUDGE JUDGE Cost. Petitions No.D-1697 of 2021 etc. Page 2 of 2 Ahmad
